---
title: "Can I exctract a jmol (javascript) object to use it offline?"
date: 2009-10-11
forum: Programming Talk
---

### Post by PC_load_letter on 2009-10-11
OK, so here is the situation:
I'm using SAGE to plot 3D interactive images. It uses jmol ([http://jmol.sourceforge.net/](http://jmol.sourceforge.net/)) to output the attached image. 

The thing is, it's an interactive image that you can rotate (in 3D), zoom in, out...etc. So, is there a way to extract this javescript object and embed it in, say, a pdf or an odp (OOo impress) file?

I can do two things to extract this object:
(1) From jmol, I get the following code, which I have no clue what to do with it.

### Post by PC_load_letter on 2009-10-15
Just for the record, if anyone else encounters the same problem. There is a stand-alone version of Jmol and you can export/import scripts to the Jmol console and run them offline (i.e without running Sage in my case).
